introduction: Server Core provides the foundational HTTP server infrastructure for the apikit library, including configuration loading, health probes, middleware, structured logging, error handling, graceful shutdown, and the handler registration API upon which all other specs depend. Note: requirements referencing Echo v4, logrus, and uuid APIs are based on PRD descriptions and should be cross-checked against the installed library versions before implementation.

glossary.APIError: The helper function `apikit.APIError(c, code, message)` that writes a standard JSON error envelope and sets `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8`.
glossary.APIGroup: The method `(*Server).APIGroup()` that returns the Echo route group mounted at the configured mount point, with `CacheNoStore` pre-applied.
glossary.Addr: The method `(*Server).Addr()` that returns the actual bound `host:port` string after the listener is established.
glossary.Build: The package-level variable `apikit.Build` (default `"dev"`) holding the short git commit SHA, overridable via `-ldflags`.
glossary.CacheCategory: An integer type identifying one of three cache behaviors: `CacheNoStore`, `CacheNoCache`, or `CachePublic`.
glossary.CacheMiddleware: The function `apikit.CacheMiddleware(cat CacheCategory) echo.MiddlewareFunc` that returns Echo middleware setting the appropriate `Cache-Control` header.
glossary.CacheNoCache: Cache category that produces `Cache-Control: no-cache`; applied to health probe endpoints.
glossary.CacheNoStore: Cache category that produces `Cache-Control: no-store`; the default for all routes under the mount point.
glossary.CachePublic: Cache category that produces `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300`; applied to the `/version` endpoint and static discovery routes.
glossary.CheckETag: The utility function `apikit.CheckETag(c, updatedAt)` that compares the `If-None-Match` request header against the resource's ETag and returns true if the client cache is current.
glossary.ETag: An HTTP response header carrying a weak cache validator derived from a resource's `updated_at` timestamp, formatted as `W/"<RFC3339-UTC-timestamp>"`.
glossary.FormatUTC: The utility function `apikit.FormatUTC(t)` that formats a `time.Time` as RFC 3339 UTC with Z suffix.
glossary.HealthChecker: A `func() error` injected into `NewServer()` that the `/readyz` endpoint calls to determine readiness; nil means always-ready.
glossary.If-None-Match: An HTTP request header used in conditional GET requests; the server returns 304 Not Modified when the header value matches the current ETag.
glossary.LoadConfig: The public function `apikit.LoadConfig()` that reads and validates `config.toml`, applying XDG resolution and defaults, and returns a `*apikit.Config`.
glossary.MaxBodyBytes: The method `(*ServerConfig).MaxBodyBytes() int64` that returns the parsed body size limit in bytes.
glossary.NewServer: The public function `apikit.NewServer(cfg, checker)` that constructs a configured Echo HTTP server from a `*apikit.Config`.
glossary.NowUTC: The utility function `apikit.NowUTC()` that returns the current time formatted as RFC 3339 UTC with Z suffix.
glossary.ParseUTC: The utility function `apikit.ParseUTC(s)` that parses any RFC 3339 timestamp (including offset variants) and normalizes it to UTC.
glossary.SetETag: The utility function `apikit.SetETag(c, updatedAt)` that sets a weak ETag header derived from a `time.Time` timestamp.
glossary.Shutdown: The method `(*Server).Shutdown(ctx)` that initiates graceful server shutdown with a fixed 15-second drain timeout.
glossary.Start: The blocking method `(*Server).Start()` that binds the listener and serves requests until shutdown.
glossary.TokenPrefix: The build-time configurable package-level variable `apikit.TokenPrefix` (default `"ak"`) used as a token prefix across all consuming components.
glossary.Version: The package-level variable `apikit.Version` (default `"dev"`) holding the semantic version string, overridable via `-ldflags`.
glossary.X-Request-ID: An HTTP header carrying the UUID v4 request identifier, set on every response and optionally supplied by the client.
glossary.XDG_CONFIG_HOME: The XDG Base Directory environment variable that, when set, redirects config loading to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/apikit/config.toml`.
glossary.XDG_DATA_HOME: The XDG Base Directory environment variable that, when set, resolves the default database path to `$XDG_DATA_HOME/apikit/apikit.db`.
glossary.apikit: The root Go module (`github.com/txsvc/apikit`) that exposes the public server API to consuming projects.
glossary.cmd/apikit: The minimal reference binary at `cmd/apikit/` that demonstrates the three-step caller usage contract and compiles to `bin/apikit`.
glossary.config.toml: The TOML-format configuration file read by `LoadConfig()` from the current directory or XDG path.
glossary.drainTimeout: The named compile-time constant `drainTimeout = 15 * time.Second` that bounds the graceful shutdown drain window.
glossary.external_url: The TOML config field `[server] external_url` storing the public URL for OAuth redirect URIs; stored as-is with no validation by Server Core.
glossary.internal/config: The internal Go package (`github.com/txsvc/apikit/internal/config`) that implements config loading; its types and functions are re-exported through the root `apikit` package.
glossary.max_body_size: The TOML config field `[server] max_body_size` specifying the maximum allowed request body size (e.g. `1MB`).
glossary.mount_point: The configurable URL prefix (default `/api/v1`) under which all consumer API routes are registered.
glossary.sync.Once: A Go standard library primitive used to implement idempotent one-time execution, used here to ensure `Shutdown()` executes its shutdown logic only once.
glossary.\"0MB\": Invalid max_body_size value (zero is not allowed)
glossary.\"2026-07-17T14:30:00Z\": Example RFC 3339 UTC timestamp with Z suffix
glossary.\"build\": \"dev\": Default build field value in /version response
glossary.\"dev\": Default value for Version and Build variables when not set via ldflags
glossary.\"version\": \"dev\": Default version field value in /version response
glossary.(nil, error): Return value when LoadConfig() encounters malformed TOML
glossary.(time.Time, nil): Return value of ParseUTC on successful parse
glossary.(time.Time{}, error): Return value of ParseUTC on parse failure
glossary.*apikit.Config: Pointer to the re-exported Config type in the root apikit package
glossary.*echo.Group: Echo route group type returned by APIGroup()
glossary.*internal/config.Config: Pointer to the internal config.Config struct
glossary.-ldflags: Go linker flags for injecting build-time variables
glossary../config.toml: Default config file path in working directory
glossary../data/apikit.db: Default database file path relative to working directory
glossary.0: Integer zero (no expiry or zero value)
glossary.1MB: Default maximum request body size (1 megabyte)
glossary.APIError(): Helper function that returns JSON error responses in the standard envelope
glossary.APIGroup(): Method returning the Echo route group at the configured mount point
glossary.Addr(): Method returning the actual bound address after Start() begins listening
glossary.BUILD: Makefile variable for build commit SHA
glossary.Cache-Control: HTTP response header controlling caching behavior
glossary.Cache-Control: no-cache: Cache-Control header value for health probe endpoints
glossary.Cache-Control: no-store: Cache-Control header value for mutable resource endpoints
glossary.Cache-Control: public, max-age=300: Cache-Control header value for static discovery endpoints
glossary.CacheMiddleware(CacheNoStore): Middleware constructor call for no-store caching
glossary.CacheMiddleware(CachePublic): Middleware constructor call for public caching
glossary.Config.Database.Path: Database path field in the Config struct
glossary.Config.Logging.Level: Logging level field in the Config struct
glossary.Config.Server.ExternalURL: External URL field in the ServerConfig struct
glossary.Config.Server.MaxBodyBytes(): Getter for parsed max body size in bytes
glossary.Content-Type: HTTP header specifying the media type of the request/response body
glossary.Content-Type: application/json; charset=: Required Content-Type header value for all responses
glossary.FormatUTC(): Utility function that formats a time.Time as RFC 3339 UTC with Z suffix
glossary.GB: Gigabyte suffix for max_body_size config value
glossary.KB: Kilobyte suffix for max_body_size config value
glossary.LoadConfig(): Function that loads config.toml respecting XDG paths and returns Config
glossary.MB: Megabyte suffix for max_body_size config value
glossary.NewServer(): Constructor that creates a configured Echo server from Config
glossary.ParseUTC(): Utility function that parses RFC 3339 timestamps and normalizes to UTC
glossary.Shutdown(): Method that gracefully stops the server with drain timeout
glossary.Shutdown(ctx): Shutdown method with context parameter for external cancellation
glossary.Start(): Method that begins HTTP listening and blocks until shutdown
glossary.W/\"<RFC3339-UTC-timestamp>\": Weak ETag format derived from updated_at timestamp
glossary.X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff: Security header preventing MIME type sniffing
glossary.X-Frame-Options: DENY: Security header preventing clickjacking via framing
glossary.Z: UTC timezone suffix in RFC 3339 timestamps
glossary.[logging] level: TOML config key for log level setting
glossary.[server] bind: TOML config key for bind address setting
glossary.[server] external_url: TOML config key for external URL setting
glossary.[server] max_body_size: TOML config key for maximum request body size
glossary.[server] port: TOML config key for HTTP listen port
glossary.apikit.Build: Package-level variable for build commit SHA, overridable via ldflags
glossary.apikit.LoadConfig(): Public wrapper that delegates to internal/config.Load()
glossary.apikit.NewServer(cfg, nil): Example call creating a server with no health checker
glossary.apikit.Version: Package-level variable for version string, overridable via ldflags
glossary.application/json: JSON MIME type required for request Content-Type
glossary.application/json; charset=utf-8: JSON MIME type with charset set on all responses
glossary.build: Field in /version response containing build commit SHA
glossary.c.JSON(): Echo context method for writing JSON responses
glossary.context.WithTimeout(ctx, drainTimeout): Go stdlib call wrapping shutdown context with drain timeout
glossary.debug: Log level for verbose debugging output
glossary.duration: Structured log field for request duration in float64 milliseconds
glossary.error: Go error type / log level for error conditions
glossary.fatal: Log level for fatal errors that terminate the process
glossary.git describe --tags: Git command to get the nearest tag for version
glossary.git rev-parse --short HEAD: Git command to get the short commit SHA for build
glossary.github.com/txsvc/apikit.Build: Full import path for the Build variable (ldflags target)
glossary.github.com/txsvc/apikit.TokenPrefix: Full import path for the TokenPrefix variable (ldflags target)
glossary.github.com/txsvc/apikit.Version: Full import path for the Version variable (ldflags target)
Go test command to run all tests in the module
glossary.host:port: Address format returned by Addr() method
glossary.info: Default log level for standard operational messages
glossary.internal/config.Load(): Internal function that reads and parses config.toml
glossary.make test: Makefile target that runs the Go test suite
glossary.method: Structured log field for HTTP request method
glossary.middleware.Recover(): Custom panic recovery middleware returning standard error envelope
glossary.nil: Go nil value (null pointer or absent value)
glossary.os.Exit(): Go stdlib function to terminate the process (not called by server library)
glossary.panic: Go panic / structured log field for panic value
glossary.path: Structured log field for HTTP request path
glossary.request_id: Structured log field for the X-Request-ID value
glossary.server.Start(): Example call to start the HTTP server
glossary.stack_trace: Structured log field for panic stack trace
glossary.status: Structured log field for HTTP response status code
glossary.time.Time: Go stdlib type representing a point in time
glossary.time.Time{}: Zero value of time.Time (returned by ParseUTC on error)
glossary.trace: Log level for most verbose output
glossary.type Config = config.Config: Go type alias re-exporting internal Config type
glossary.updatedAt: Resource timestamp used to derive ETag values
glossary.uuid.Parse(): UUID parsing function used to validate incoming X-Request-ID values
glossary.var Build = \"dev\": Package-level Build variable declaration with default value
glossary.var TokenPrefix = \"ak\": Package-level TokenPrefix variable declaration with default value
glossary.var Version = \"dev\": Package-level Version variable declaration with default value
glossary.version: Field in /version response containing version string
glossary.{\"status\": \"not ready\"}: Response body for /readyz when database is unreachable
glossary.{\"status\": \"ready\"}: Response body for /readyz when database is reachable

user_story.goal: follow a simple three-step pattern to bring up an HTTP server
user_story.benefit: I can integrate apikit without reading implementation details

acceptance_criteria.action: exposes `LoadConfig()`, `NewServer()`, `APIGroup()`, and `Start()` as its primary public API in the root module
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Each function is callable from external packages importing `github.com/txsvc/apikit`

acceptance_criteria.action: reads and validates `config.toml`, applies XDG resolution and defaults, and returns `(*apikit.Config, error)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `(*Config, nil)` on success; `(nil, error)` on malformed or invalid config
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called by a consuming project

acceptance_criteria.action: never calls `LoadConfig()` internally, performs no file I/O, and never returns a config-related error
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns a fully constructed `*Server` ready for handler registration

acceptance_criteria.action: panics immediately with a descriptive message identifying the nil config as a programming error
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: panic (process terminates unless recovered by test harness); error message must name `cfg` as nil
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a nil `cfg` argument

acceptance_criteria.action: blocks until the server shuts down via signal or external `Shutdown()` call, then returns
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `nil` on clean graceful shutdown; non-nil error on bind failure or other network-level failure
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called on a `*Server` instance

edge_cases.action: panics with a descriptive message; does not call `os.Exit()` or return a zero-value `*Server`
edge_cases.return_contract: panic value is a non-empty string identifying the nil config

edge_cases.action: signals the error via return value or panic (programming error only); never calls `os.Exit()` — process termination is the caller's responsibility
edge_cases.return_contract: error returned or panic raised; `os.Exit()` is never called by library code
edge_cases.error_condition: any error condition arises inside `NewServer()`, `APIGroup()`, or `Start()`

user_story.goal: have a minimal compilable reference binary that exercises the three-step caller usage contract
user_story.benefit: I can smoke-test the library wiring manually and verify the build is sound

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `apikit.LoadConfig()`, then `apikit.NewServer(cfg, nil)`, then `server.Start()`, registering no additional handlers beyond the built-in endpoints
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Binary compiles successfully via `make build` and produces `bin/apikit`

acceptance_criteria.action: responds with HTTP 200 and `{"status": "ok"}`, confirming library wiring is correct
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with JSON body `{"status": "ok"}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: started and a GET request is made to `/healthz`

acceptance_criteria.action: is verified for compilation by `go test ./...` as part of `make test`; it does not have dedicated functional test cases beyond what integration tests cover for built-in endpoints
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `go test ./...` exits 0 when the binary compiles

user_story.goal: configure the server via a TOML file with sensible defaults
user_story.benefit: I can run the server with zero configuration for development and override values for production

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a `*Config` populated entirely with defaults and a nil error; does not attempt to fall back to any other path
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `(*Config, nil)` with all fields at their specified defaults
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `config.toml` is absent from the resolved config path

acceptance_criteria.action: returns `(nil, error)` with a descriptive parse error; does not apply defaults
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `(nil, error)` where error describes the TOML parse failure
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `config.toml` exists but contains invalid TOML syntax

acceptance_criteria.action: applies the following defaults when fields are absent: port=8080, bind=`"0.0.0.0"`, external_url=`""`, mount_point=`"/api/v1"`, max_body_size=`"1MB"`, database path=`"./data/apikit.db"`, logging level=`"info"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returned `*Config` fields match the documented defaults for any absent key

acceptance_criteria.action: validates `[server] port` is an integer in range 0–65535; port 0 is valid
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `(nil, error)` when port is out of range; `(*Config, nil)` when valid including 0

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts any non-empty string for `[server] bind` without format validation; replaces an empty or absent bind value with the default `"0.0.0.0"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Non-empty bind value stored as-is; empty replaced with `"0.0.0.0"`; no error returned for non-empty values

acceptance_criteria.action: stores `[server] external_url` as-is with no validation; when absent or empty, stores `""` in `Config.Server.ExternalURL` and returns nil error
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `Config.Server.ExternalURL` equals the raw string from config or `""` when absent; no error

acceptance_criteria.action: validates `[logging] level` is one of `trace`, `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`, `fatal`, `panic` (case-insensitive); invalid values return `(nil, error)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `(nil, error)` for invalid log level; `(*Config, nil)` for valid

acceptance_criteria.action: validates `[server] max_body_size` must be a positive integer immediately followed by `KB`, `MB`, or `GB` (case-insensitive, no spaces); an empty string is treated as absent and the default `1MB` is applied with no error; any other invalid value returns `(nil, error)`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Parsed byte value stored internally; `MaxBodyBytes()` returns the correct int64

acceptance_criteria.action: has no filesystem side effects beyond reading the config file — no directory creation, no file writes, no path existence checks
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: No files or directories are created or modified as a result of calling `LoadConfig()`

edge_cases.action: returns `(nil, error)` because zero and negative sizes are invalid
edge_cases.return_contract: `(nil, error)` with message indicating invalid size
edge_cases.error_condition: `max_body_size` has a value of `"0MB"` or a negative integer prefix

edge_cases.action: returns `(nil, error)` with a descriptive error message
edge_cases.error_condition: `max_body_size` uses an unsupported suffix such as `TB` or contains a space between number and suffix

edge_cases.action: returns `(nil, error)` with a descriptive range error
edge_cases.return_contract: `(nil, error)` naming the out-of-range port value

edge_cases.action: returns `(nil, error)` listing the accepted level values
edge_cases.return_contract: `(nil, error)` with message listing valid levels
edge_cases.error_condition: an unrecognized `[logging] level` string is provided

user_story.goal: have config and data paths follow XDG conventions when environment variables are set
user_story.benefit: apikit integrates naturally with Linux desktop and server directory standards

acceptance_criteria.action: loads config exclusively from `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/apikit/config.toml`; does not fall back to `./config.toml` if the XDG path does not exist
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Defaults applied with nil error when XDG file is absent; parsed config returned when file exists

acceptance_criteria.action: resolves the default database path to `$XDG_DATA_HOME/apikit/apikit.db` and stores it in `Config.Database.Path`; does not create any directories
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `Config.Database.Path` equals `$XDG_DATA_HOME/apikit/apikit.db`; no directory created

acceptance_criteria.action: looks for `config.toml` in the current working directory and uses `./data/apikit.db` as the default database path
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Config path is `./config.toml`; database default path is `./data/apikit.db`

edge_cases.action: applies all defaults and returns nil error; does not attempt to read `./config.toml`
edge_cases.state: `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set but `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/apikit/config.toml` does not exist

user_story.goal: have reliable liveness and readiness probe endpoints at well-known paths
user_story.benefit: the platform can automatically restart unhealthy pods and gate traffic to ready pods

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 200 with JSON body `{"status": "ok"}` and `Cache-Control: no-cache`; never requires authentication
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200, `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8`, body `{"status": "ok"}`, `Cache-Control: no-cache`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: GET request is received at `/healthz`

acceptance_criteria.action: calls the `HealthChecker`; returns HTTP 200 `{"status": "ready"}` if it returns nil, or HTTP 503 `{"status": "not ready"}` if it returns an error
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 body `{"status": "ready"}` on nil; HTTP 503 body `{"status": "not ready"}` on error; both include `Cache-Control: no-cache`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: GET request is received at `/readyz` and a `HealthChecker` was provided to `NewServer()`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 200 `{"status": "ready"}` without calling any external check
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 `{"status": "ready"}` with `Cache-Control: no-cache`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: GET request is received at `/readyz` and no `HealthChecker` was provided (nil)

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 200 with JSON body containing `version` (from `apikit.Version`), `build` (from `apikit.Build`), and `mount_point` (from config); sets `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200, body `{"version": "<string>", "build": "<string>", "mount_point": "<string>"}`, `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: GET request is received at `/version`

acceptance_criteria.action: registers `/healthz`, `/readyz`, and `/version` at the server root, outside the `mount_point`, so they are reachable without authentication regardless of mount point configuration
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Endpoints reachable at `/healthz`, `/readyz`, `/version` even when `mount_point` is not `/`

edge_cases.action: returns `"version": "dev"` and `"build": "dev"` in the response body
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 200 with `version` and `build` both equal to `"dev"`
edge_cases.trigger: GET `/version` is called when `apikit.Version` and `apikit.Build` have not been overridden by `-ldflags`

edge_cases.action: returns an empty string for the corresponding field in the `/version` response without substituting any fallback value
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 200 with the overridden field equal to `""`
edge_cases.trigger: `apikit.Version` or `apikit.Build` is overridden to an empty string via `-ldflags`

user_story.goal: have the server drain in-flight requests before exiting on SIGTERM or SIGINT
user_story.benefit: active requests complete cleanly and no responses are truncated during deployments or restarts

acceptance_criteria.action: stops accepting new connections, waits up to `drainTimeout` (15 seconds) for in-flight requests to complete, logs shutdown initiation at `info` level, then force-closes
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `Start()` returns `nil` after clean drain; `Start()` returns non-nil if force-close was needed due to timeout
acceptance_criteria.trigger: SIGTERM or SIGINT is received

acceptance_criteria.action: defines `drainTimeout` as a named compile-time constant equal to `15 * time.Second`; it is not user-configurable
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Constant is named `drainTimeout` in the server package and equals 15 seconds

acceptance_criteria.action: internally derives a context via `context.WithTimeout(ctx, drainTimeout)` and passes it to Echo's shutdown; the earlier of the caller's context deadline and the 15-second timeout wins
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `nil` after clean drain; returns error if force-closed
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called externally (e.g. from a test goroutine)

acceptance_criteria.action: is idempotent using `sync.Once` or equivalent atomic guard; the first call initiates shutdown, all subsequent calls return `nil` immediately without blocking
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Every call to `Shutdown()` returns `nil`; no call blocks after the first completes

acceptance_criteria.action: logs shutdown initiation at `info` level, including the `drainTimeout` duration
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Log entry at `info` level is emitted before drain window begins

acceptance_criteria.action: does not call `os.Exit()` during shutdown; `Start()` returns to its caller after shutdown completes
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `Start()` returns normally; calling process is not terminated by the library

edge_cases.action: proceeds to force-close immediately without waiting for the drain timeout; `Start()` returns promptly
edge_cases.return_contract: Force-close occurs at context cancellation; `Start()` returns non-nil error or nil depending on drain state
edge_cases.error_condition: caller's context is cancelled before the 15-second `drainTimeout` elapses

edge_cases.action: force-closes all connections after the timeout; `Start()` returns
edge_cases.return_contract: `Start()` returns after force-close; remaining connections are terminated
edge_cases.error_condition: in-flight requests do not complete within the 15-second `drainTimeout`

edge_cases.action: only the first call executes the shutdown sequence; all others return `nil` immediately; no data race or deadlock occurs
edge_cases.return_contract: All concurrent calls return `nil`; server shuts down exactly once
edge_cases.trigger: called concurrently from multiple goroutines simultaneously

edge_cases.action: returns a non-nil error immediately with a message indicating the server has already been shut down; does not attempt to rebind or reinitialize
edge_cases.return_contract: Non-nil error returned immediately; error message indicates server is already shut down
edge_cases.trigger: called a second time on the same `*Server` instance after shutdown has completed

user_story.goal: start the server on an OS-assigned port and discover the address programmatically
user_story.benefit: tests run in parallel without port collisions in CI environments

acceptance_criteria.action: binds to an OS-assigned ephemeral port; after the listener is established, `Addr()` returns the actual `host:port` string
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `Addr()` returns a non-empty `host:port` string such as `"0.0.0.0:54321"`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an empty string
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `""` (empty string)
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called before `Start()` has bound the listener or after the server has shut down

user_story.goal: have a deterministic and documented middleware chain applied to every request
user_story.benefit: middleware interactions are predictable and error responses from early middleware include context set by earlier middleware

acceptance_criteria.action: applies middleware in the fixed order: (1) Panic Recovery, (2) Request ID, (3) Body Size Limit, (4) Content-Type Enforcement, (5) Security Headers, (6) Logging, (7) Handler
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Order is deterministic for every request reaching a registered handler

acceptance_criteria.action: ensures that a 413 (Payload Too Large) response includes the `X-Request-ID` header assigned in step 2
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: 413 response carries `X-Request-ID` header

acceptance_criteria.action: ensures the structured log entry always captures the actual final HTTP status code, including error codes produced by middleware steps 1–5
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Log entry `status` field matches the status code sent to the client

user_story.goal: have any unhandled panic in a handler or middleware caught and returned as a structured JSON 500 error
user_story.benefit: panics do not crash the server and every error response follows the standard envelope

acceptance_criteria.action: recovers the panic, logs the panic value and full stack trace at `error` level with logrus using structured fields `request_id`, `panic`, and `stack_trace`; returns HTTP 500 via `APIError()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 500 JSON error envelope with `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8`; panic is not re-raised
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a panic occurs in any downstream middleware or handler

acceptance_criteria.action: uses a custom panic recovery middleware; Echo's built-in `middleware.Recover()` is explicitly disabled to prevent duplicate recovery and inconsistent error responses
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Only one recovery layer is active; all panic responses use `APIError()` envelope

edge_cases.action: includes the assigned `request_id` in the structured error log entry
edge_cases.return_contract: Log entry contains `request_id` field matching the value set in the `X-Request-ID` response header
edge_cases.trigger: a panic occurs after the `X-Request-ID` has been assigned

user_story.goal: have a unique UUID v4 request ID on every request and response
user_story.benefit: I can correlate log entries with client-reported request IDs for debugging

acceptance_criteria.action: generates a new UUID v4 and sets it in the `X-Request-ID` response header and the structured log entry
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `X-Request-ID` response header is a valid UUID v4 string
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an incoming request does not contain an `X-Request-ID` header

acceptance_criteria.action: parses the value using `uuid.Parse()`; if version is 4, reuses it as the request ID in the response header and log entry
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `X-Request-ID` response header echoes the client-supplied UUID v4
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an incoming request contains an `X-Request-ID` header with a valid UUID v4 value

acceptance_criteria.action: discards the client-supplied value and generates a new UUID v4; sets it in the `X-Request-ID` response header and log entry
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `X-Request-ID` response header is a freshly generated UUID v4, not the invalid client value
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an incoming request contains an `X-Request-ID` header with a value that is not a valid UUID v4

user_story.goal: have every HTTP request produce a structured JSON log entry with consistent fields
user_story.benefit: I can query and aggregate request logs using standard log aggregation tools

acceptance_criteria.action: uses logrus configured for JSON output; every HTTP request produces a log entry with fields: `method` (string), `path` (string), `status` (integer), `duration` (float64 milliseconds), `request_id` (string)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Log line is valid JSON with all five fields present and correctly typed

acceptance_criteria.action: logs the request at `debug` level regardless of the configured log level; entry includes all five standard fields including `duration` as float64
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Log entry emitted at `debug` level; absent from output when configured log level is `info` or above

acceptance_criteria.action: configures logrus log level from `Config.Logging.Level` at server startup
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Log entries below the configured level are not emitted

user_story.goal: receive a clear error when I send a non-JSON body in a POST, PUT, or PATCH request
user_story.benefit: I know immediately that my client is misconfigured rather than receiving a cryptic parse error

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 415 (Unsupported Media Type) via `APIError()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 415, `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8`, standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST, PUT, or PATCH request arrives with a `Content-Type` header other than `application/json`

acceptance_criteria.action: does not apply to GET, DELETE, HEAD, or OPTIONS requests; only POST, PUT, and PATCH are checked
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Non-mutating methods pass through without Content-Type inspection

user_story.goal: enforce a maximum request body size to prevent oversized payloads from consuming server resources
user_story.benefit: the server is protected against large-body denial-of-service payloads

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 413 (Payload Too Large) via `APIError()` before any further processing
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 413, `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8`, standard JSON error envelope; includes `X-Request-ID` header
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a request body exceeds the configured `max_body_size`

acceptance_criteria.action: applies the limit from `Config.Server.MaxBodyBytes()`, defaulting to 1,048,576 bytes (1MB) when not configured
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Limit is enforced at the configured byte value

user_story.goal: have standard HTTP security headers applied to every response automatically
user_story.benefit: the server is protected against MIME sniffing, clickjacking, and referrer leakage without per-handler configuration

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, and `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer` on every response
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: All three headers are present on every response regardless of route or status code

acceptance_criteria.action: does NOT set `Cache-Control`; that header is managed exclusively by `CacheMiddleware`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: No `Cache-Control` header is set by the Security Headers middleware

user_story.goal: receive appropriate Cache-Control headers on every response based on endpoint category
user_story.benefit: caching intermediaries and clients handle responses correctly without manual header management

acceptance_criteria.action: pre-applies `CacheMiddleware(CacheNoStore)` to the returned Echo group so all consumer-registered routes default to `Cache-Control: no-store`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Any handler registered on the group without explicit cache middleware returns `Cache-Control: no-store`

acceptance_criteria.action: that route returns `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300`, overriding the group-level `CacheNoStore` default
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300` on the specific route; other group routes still return `no-store`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a consumer attaches `CacheMiddleware(CachePublic)` to a specific route registered on `APIGroup()`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns Echo middleware that sets exactly one of: `Cache-Control: no-store` (`CacheNoStore`), `Cache-Control: no-cache` (`CacheNoCache`), or `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300` (`CachePublic`) based on the `CacheCategory` argument
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Header value matches the category constant exactly

user_story.goal: use conditional GET requests to avoid re-downloading unchanged resources
user_story.benefit: bandwidth and latency are reduced for polling clients

acceptance_criteria.action: sets the `ETag` response header to `W/"<RFC3339-UTC-timestamp>"` derived from `updatedAt` formatted via `FormatUTC()`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `ETag` header value matches the format `W/"2026-07-17T14:30:00Z"`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called by a handler with a non-zero `updatedAt` time

acceptance_criteria.action: returns `true`; the handler is expected to return HTTP 304 Not Modified with no body
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `true`; HTTP 304 is issued by the handler
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called by a handler with a non-zero `updatedAt` time and the request includes `If-None-Match` matching the current ETag

acceptance_criteria.action: returns `false`; handler proceeds normally
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Returns `false`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a non-zero `updatedAt` time and the request `If-None-Match` does not match

acceptance_criteria.action: is a no-op: `SetETag` sets no `ETag` header; `CheckETag` returns `false` without checking `If-None-Match`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: No `ETag` header set by `SetETag`; `false` returned by `CheckETag`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with the zero value of `time.Time`

user_story.goal: receive a consistent JSON error structure for all error responses
user_story.benefit: I can parse errors with a single schema regardless of which layer produced them

acceptance_criteria.action: writes a JSON body `{"error": {"code": <integer>, "message": "<string>"}}` and sets `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8` on the response; returns the error from `c.JSON()` directly
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Response body matches the envelope schema; `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8`; return value propagates write errors

acceptance_criteria.action: all middleware-generated error responses (HTTP 413, 415, 500) use `APIError()` to ensure consistent envelope and Content-Type
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Every error response from middleware includes `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8` and the standard envelope

user_story.goal: have utility functions that produce and parse RFC 3339 UTC timestamps with Z suffix
user_story.benefit: all API responses use a consistent timestamp format without manual conversion in each handler

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the current time formatted as an RFC 3339 UTC string with Z suffix (e.g. `"2026-07-17T14:30:00Z"`)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: String ends with `Z`; conforms to RFC 3339

acceptance_criteria.action: formats the given `time.Time` as RFC 3339 UTC with Z suffix, converting to UTC if necessary
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: String ends with `Z`; represents the equivalent UTC time

acceptance_criteria.action: parses the string and normalizes the result to UTC; returns `(time.Time, nil)` with the UTC-equivalent time
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `time.Time` is in UTC location; `.UTC()` call returns same value
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with a valid RFC 3339 string including one with a non-UTC timezone offset

acceptance_criteria.action: returns `(time.Time{}, error)` where `time.Time{}` is the zero value
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `(time.Time{}, error)`; error is non-nil and describes the parse failure
acceptance_criteria.trigger: called with an invalid or non-RFC-3339 string

user_story.goal: register my own Echo handlers under the configured mount point and inherit all middleware
user_story.benefit: I can extend the API surface without reimplementing middleware or wiring

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the same pre-constructed `*echo.Group` on every call; never returns nil and never panics, even when called after `Shutdown()` has completed
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Non-nil `*echo.Group` with `mount_point` as its prefix

acceptance_criteria.action: automatically inherit the full middleware chain (Panic Recovery, Request ID, Body Size Limit, Content-Type Enforcement, Security Headers, Logging) in the documented order
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: All middleware is applied to every handler registered on the group

acceptance_criteria.action: is safe to call from multiple goroutines concurrently after `NewServer()` has returned; all route registration should be completed before `Start()` is called
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: No data race on concurrent `APIGroup()` calls

edge_cases.action: returns the pre-constructed Echo group without panicking or returning nil; routes registered at this point may never receive traffic
edge_cases.return_contract: Non-nil `*echo.Group` returned; documented in godoc that post-shutdown registration is a no-op for traffic purposes

user_story.goal: override `Version`, `Build`, and `TokenPrefix` at compile time via `-ldflags`
user_story.benefit: I can embed my own version and token namespace into the binary without forking the library

acceptance_criteria.action: declares `var Version = "dev"`, `var Build = "dev"`, and `var TokenPrefix = "ak"` as package-level variables overridable via `-ldflags` targeting `github.com/txsvc/apikit.Version`, `github.com/txsvc/apikit.Build`, and `github.com/txsvc/apikit.TokenPrefix` respectively
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Variables are accessible as `apikit.Version`, `apikit.Build`, `apikit.TokenPrefix`

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `VERSION` via `git describe --tags` and `BUILD` via `git rev-parse --short HEAD`, falling back to `"dev"` when git commands fail; injects both into the binary via `-ldflags`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Binary built in a git repository has non-`"dev"` version and build values; binary built outside git repository has `"dev"` values without build failure

user_story.goal: use `apikit.Config` and `apikit.LoadConfig()` without importing internal packages
user_story.benefit: I never need to reference `internal/config` directly and the public API remains stable

acceptance_criteria.action: declares `type Config = config.Config` as a type alias so `*apikit.Config` and `*internal/config.Config` are identical types requiring no conversion
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `*apikit.Config` is directly passable to `NewServer()` without type assertion or conversion

acceptance_criteria.action: is a thin re-export of `internal/config.Load()` with identical semantics; external consumers never need to import `internal/config` directly
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Calling `apikit.LoadConfig()` produces the same result as calling `internal/config.Load()` directly

correctness_properties.title: Middleware Chain Completeness
correctness_properties.for_any: HTTP request reaching a registered handler
correctness_properties.invariant: all six middleware steps (Panic Recovery, Request ID, Body Size Limit, Content-Type Enforcement, Security Headers, Logging) are applied in documented order before and after the handler executes

correctness_properties.title: Error Envelope Consistency
correctness_properties.for_any: HTTP error response produced by any middleware or handler
correctness_properties.invariant: the response body conforms to `{"error": {"code": <integer>, "message": "<string>"}}` and `Content-Type` is `application/json; charset=utf-8`

correctness_properties.title: Request ID Propagation
correctness_properties.invariant: `X-Request-ID` header is present and contains a UUID v4 string; the same value appears in the structured log entry for the corresponding request

correctness_properties.title: Security Headers Universality
correctness_properties.invariant: `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, and `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer` are all present and `Cache-Control` is absent from the Security Headers middleware output

correctness_properties.title: Shutdown Idempotency
correctness_properties.for_any: sequence of one or more concurrent or sequential calls to `Shutdown()`
correctness_properties.invariant: the server shuts down exactly once; all calls to `Shutdown()` return nil; no call blocks after the first shutdown completes; no data race occurs

correctness_properties.title: Config Defaults Completeness
correctness_properties.invariant: all `*Config` fields are set to their documented defaults: port=8080, bind=`"0.0.0.0"`, external_url=`""`, mount_point=`"/api/v1"`, max_body_size equivalent to 1MB, database path=`"./data/apikit.db"`, logging level=`"info"`

correctness_properties.title: UTC Timestamp Normalization
correctness_properties.for_any: call to `FormatUTC()` or `ParseUTC()` with a valid time value
correctness_properties.invariant: the resulting string or returned `time.Time` represents the equivalent UTC instant with no timezone offset; formatted strings end with the `Z` suffix

correctness_properties.title: Library Non-Termination
correctness_properties.for_any: error condition arising inside any apikit library function except `NewServer()` with nil cfg
correctness_properties.invariant: the library never calls `os.Exit()`; errors are signaled exclusively via return values so the calling process retains control

correctness_properties.title: Drain Timeout is the Earlier Deadline
correctness_properties.for_any: call to `Shutdown(ctx)` with any caller-supplied context
correctness_properties.invariant: force-close occurs at whichever fires first: cancellation of the caller's context or expiry of the 15-second `drainTimeout`; neither source can cause force-close to be delayed beyond the `drainTimeout`

correctness_properties.title: Health Probe Log Suppression at Info Level
correctness_properties.for_any: GET request to `/healthz` or `/readyz` when configured log level is `info` or above
correctness_properties.invariant: no log entry is emitted for the health probe request at `info` level or above; when log level is `debug` or `trace`, a structurally identical log entry is emitted

steps.action: starts the binary without a `config.toml` present
detects no config file, applies all defaults, returns `(*Config, nil)`
constructs the Echo server with default config and nil `HealthChecker`; registers middleware chain and built-in routes
binds to `0.0.0.0:8080`, begins serving requests
accepts incoming connections and routes them through the middleware chain to registered handlers

execution_paths.title: Kubernetes Readiness Probe with Injected HealthChecker

steps.action: calls `NewServer(cfg, dbHealthChecker)` where `dbHealthChecker` is a `func() error` that pings the database
binds and begins serving
sends GET `/readyz`
routes request through middleware chain; Logging middleware will emit at `debug` level
calls the injected `HealthChecker`
returns nil (database reachable)
returns HTTP 200 `{"status": "ready"}` with `Cache-Control: no-cache`
sends POST request with body larger than `max_body_size`
assigns a UUID v4 and sets `X-Request-ID`
detects body exceeds `MaxBodyBytes()` limit
writes HTTP 413 JSON envelope with `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8`
records log entry with `status: 413` and the assigned `request_id`
receives HTTP 413 with standard error envelope and `X-Request-ID` header
sends SIGTERM to the running server process
calls `Shutdown(context.Background())`
uses `sync.Once` to ensure one-time execution; logs shutdown initiation at `info` level; derives context with `drainTimeout` (15 seconds)
stops accepting new connections; drains in-flight requests
complete normally and produce standard structured log entries
returns `nil` after drain completes
receives nil from `Start()` and exits cleanly
creates a `*Config` with `Port: 0` directly (no file I/O needed)
calls `NewServer(cfg, nil)` and launches `server.Start()` in a goroutine
binds to an OS-assigned ephemeral port
calls `server.Addr()` to obtain the actual `host:port`
constructs request URL from `Addr()` and sends GET `/healthz`
returns HTTP 200 `{"status": "ok"}`
calls `server.Shutdown(context.Background())` to stop the server
returns nil; test goroutine completes
sends a GET request that triggers a registered handler
assigns UUID v4 as `request_id`
panics with an unhandled error
recovers the panic value; logs `error` level entry with `request_id`, `panic`, and `stack_trace` fields
writes HTTP 500 JSON envelope with `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8`
records log entry with `status: 500` and `request_id`
receives HTTP 500 standard error envelope; server continues serving

execution_paths.title: Conditional GET with ETag — 304 Not Modified

steps.action: sends GET request for a resource
retrieves resource with `updated_at` timestamp; calls `SetETag(c, updatedAt)` to set `ETag: W/"2026-07-17T14:30:00Z"`
receives HTTP 200 with `ETag` header; stores ETag value
sends subsequent GET with `If-None-Match: W/"2026-07-17T14:30:00Z"`
calls `CheckETag(c, updatedAt)`; ETag matches `If-None-Match`
returns `true`
returns HTTP 304 Not Modified with no body
uses cached response; no body transferred

execution_paths.title: Consumer Route Registration with Cache Override

steps.action: calls `apikit.LoadConfig()` and `apikit.NewServer(cfg, nil)`
calls `server.APIGroup()` to obtain the Echo group at `mount_point`
registers `api.GET("/providers", listProviders, apikit.CacheMiddleware(apikit.CachePublic))`
calls `server.Start()`
sends GET `<mount_point>/providers`
applies per-route `CacheMiddleware(CachePublic)` which overrides the group-level `CacheNoStore`
returns HTTP 200 with `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300`

error_handling.condition: `config.toml` exists but contains malformed TOML syntax
error_handling.behavior: `LoadConfig()` returns `(nil, error)` with a descriptive parse error; server does not start
`LoadConfig()` returns `(nil, error)` naming the invalid port value; server does not start

error_handling.condition: `[logging] level` is not one of the accepted values
error_handling.behavior: `LoadConfig()` returns `(nil, error)` listing valid levels; server does not start

error_handling.condition: `[server] max_body_size` has an invalid non-empty value (bad suffix, space, zero, negative)
error_handling.behavior: `LoadConfig()` returns `(nil, error)` describing the format error; server does not start

error_handling.condition: The listen port is already in use when `Start()` is called
error_handling.behavior: `Start()` returns a non-nil error; does not call `os.Exit()`; calling `main()` is responsible for handling the error

error_handling.condition: `[server] bind` contains an address the OS rejects at bind time
error_handling.behavior: `Start()` returns a non-nil error promptly; does not panic; `main()` handles the error

error_handling.condition: A handler or middleware panics during request processing
error_handling.behavior: Panic Recovery middleware recovers the panic, logs at `error` level with `request_id`, `panic`, and `stack_trace` fields, returns HTTP 500 via `APIError()`
Body Size Limit middleware returns HTTP 413 via `APIError()` before further processing; response includes `X-Request-ID` and `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8`

error_handling.condition: POST, PUT, or PATCH request has a non-JSON `Content-Type` header
error_handling.behavior: Content-Type Enforcement middleware returns HTTP 415 via `APIError()`; response includes `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8`
`NewServer()` panics immediately with a descriptive message identifying nil config as a programming error; does not return a zero-value `*Server`

error_handling.condition: `Start()` is called a second time on the same `*Server` instance after shutdown
error_handling.behavior: `Start()` returns a non-nil error immediately with a message indicating the server has already been shut down; does not rebind or reinitialize

error_handling.condition: `ParseUTC()` receives an invalid or non-RFC-3339 string
error_handling.behavior: Returns `(time.Time{}, error)` where error is non-nil; caller must check error before using the time value

error_handling.condition: In-flight requests do not complete within the 15-second `drainTimeout` during shutdown
error_handling.behavior: Server force-closes all connections after `drainTimeout` elapses; `Start()` returns

error_handling.condition: Caller's context passed to `Shutdown()` is cancelled before `drainTimeout` elapses
error_handling.behavior: Force-close proceeds immediately at context cancellation without waiting for the drain timeout

symbols.name: Echo
symbols.notes: Core HTTP framework type; used as the underlying server engine

symbols.name: Group
symbols.notes: Route group returned by `APIGroup()`; consumers register handlers on this type

symbols.name: MiddlewareFunc
symbols.notes: Function type returned by `CacheMiddleware()` and used throughout the middleware chain

symbols.name: Context
symbols.notes: Request/response context passed to handlers and `APIError()`

symbols.name: (*Echo).Shutdown
symbols.notes: Called inside `(*Server).Shutdown()` with the derived timeout context

symbols.name: Decode
symbols.signature: func Decode(data string, v interface{}) (MetaData, error)
symbols.notes: Used by `internal/config.Load()` to parse TOML config file content

symbols.name: WithFields
symbols.notes: Used to produce structured log entries with `method`, `path`, `status`, `duration`, `request_id` fields

symbols.name: SetFormatter
symbols.notes: Called at startup to configure JSON output format

symbols.name: SetLevel
symbols.notes: Called at startup to apply the configured log level from `Config.Logging.Level`

symbols.name: Parse
symbols.notes: Used in Request ID middleware to validate incoming `X-Request-ID` header values

symbols.name: New
symbols.notes: Used to generate new UUID v4 request IDs
